name: Saffron Rice and Black Beans
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley or basil OR
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse and sort the beans.
2. Put them into a heavy pot and cover with 2 cups of water.
3. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat, cover the pot, and remove from heat.
4. Set aside and allow the beans to soak for 1 to 2 hours.
5. Add onion, bay leaves, and garlic to the pot.
6. Put the pot over low heat and cook the beans, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until tender.
7. If necessary, add more water.
8. Remove and discard onion, bay leaves, and garlic when beans are cooked.
9. Keep beans warm.
10. Prepare tomato and onion garnish about 1 hour before serving.
11. Put tomatoes and onions into a serving bowl. Add olive oil, vinegar, cumin, black pepper to taste, cayenne pepper, and parsley or basil. Toss well.
12. Bring 2 cups of water to a boil in a saucepan. Add rice and saffron threads. Stir and cover the saucepan.
13. Reduce heat and cook for 18 to 20 minutes, or until rice has absorbed all water.
14. To serve, place warm rice, black beans, and garnish into separate bowls. Each diner should top a bed of rice with beans and then garnish.
